_comment: Trigger evals: for each prompt, the skill whose description should win. Routing pairs (marked route) are the hard cases — two skills plausibly match and the descriptions' routing hints must break the tie. Run per evals/README.md.

cases.prompt: I just cloned this repo and have no idea how it's structured
generate an AGENTS.md for this project
generate an architecture diagram for this codebase
write human-facing architecture docs for the payment module
create a mermaid diagram of how this feature works
document this codebase for new team members
add rate limiting to the public API
login returns 500 when the password field is empty
this test is flaky, sometimes it passes sometimes not
this module is a mess — clean up the architecture without changing behavior
where would paying down tech debt actually be worth it here?
is this ready to ship?
review and commit this
take this whole task and come back with a PR, don't ask me at every step
cases.route: autopilot is user-invoked-only; feature is the model-side answer

cases.prompt: what are people saying about Bun lately?
what's the community buzz on the new React compiler?
should we use Kafka or NATS for event streaming?
compare Postgres vs MongoDB for our analytics workload
is this job posting worth applying to?
do the sources in this report actually say what it claims?
what are similar projects shipping that we're missing?
compare our repo to LangChain — what's worth adopting?
what's on my plate today?
teach me Rust ownership from scratch
quiz me on what I learned about SQL
Claude keeps making the same mistake every session — fix my setup
why does the agent keep ignoring my CLAUDE.md rule?
